expert_rss.title: Ecofascist and Nihilistic Extremism: Taking Stock of Evolving Grievances in Targeted Violence Manifestos
expert_rss.summary: When extreme right lone actor terrorists expressed environmental concerns in the manifestos related to attacks in Christchurch (2019), El Paso (2019), and Buffalo (2022), it rightly sparked a lot of attention. The media landscape, from Vice to The Guardian, expressed interest in this phenomenon, so-called ecofascism. Within academia, the phenomenon sparked debates: was this really the perpetrators

expert_rss.title: Why Cyber Threats to Critical Infrastructure Demand a New Homeland Response Model
expert_rss.summary: The devastating flash flooding that hit central Texas in July 2025 quickly brought out thousands of local volunteers to help search over more than 60 miles for people swept away by rushing waters.   With little formal infrastructure for responding to such a widespread disaster, volunteers and local authorities relied on a civilian version of the military’s Technical Awareness Kit (CIVTAK) for oper

expert_rss.title: Winning the Cognitive Fight Requires More Than Strategy
expert_rss.summary: The operator sits in a secure basement, awaiting the green light to push out a digital expose’ onto carefully selected social media platforms and networks.

expert_rss.title: CISA, NCSC UK, and Global Partners Issue Advisory on Chinese Government-Linked Covert Cyber Networks
expert_rss.summary: The Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) and the United Kingdom’s National Cyber Security Centre (NCSC-UK), together with federal and international partners, have released a new cybersecurity advisory titled “Defending Against China-Nexus Covert Networks of Compromised Devices.”

expert_rss.title: Coast Guard Offloads Over $19M of Cocaine Interdicted in Caribbean Sea
expert_rss.summary: U.S. Coast Guard Cutter Resolute’s crew offloaded approximately 2,570 pounds of cocaine worth more than $19.3 million and transferred six suspected drug smugglers to federal authorities, Thursday, at Base Miami Beach.

expert_rss.title: U.S. House Unanimously Passes Mystic Alerts Act to Require Emergency Preparedness
expert_rss.summary: As hearings continue to be held in Austin in lawsuits filed by parents against Camp Mystic, and as a multi-state agency investigation continues, a Texas congressman’s bill to require updating a national emergency preparedness system unanimously passed the U.S. House.

expert_rss.title: FEMA Approves More Than $657 Million to Reimburse States, Medical Facilities for Backlog of Public Assistance
expert_rss.summary: Department of Homeland Security Secretary Markwayne Mullin has announced more than $657 million in FEMA funding to reimburse states, local governments and health care facilities for their costs to respond to the COVID-19 pandemic.

expert_rss.title: Over $250M in Federal Funding Announced to Help States and Local Communities Protect Against Floods
expert_rss.summary: The Federal Emergency Management Agency (FEMA) has announced more than $250 million in funding to help states prevent damage from future flood disasters.

expert_rss.title: Office of Naval Research Leaders Talk Future of Autonomy and Unmanned Innovation at Panel Discussion
expert_rss.summary: When asked about the Office of Naval Research’s (ONR) role in the development of autonomous and unmanned systems, Chief of Naval Research (CNR) Dr. Rachel Riley highlighted some of the command’s most successful investments: REMUS underwater vehicles in the 1990s and early 2000s, swarmboats in 2014, LOCUST aerial vehicles in 2015, and the Sea Hunter and Sea Hawk surface vehicles in 2017.

expert_rss.title: FEMA Approves $2 Million to Support Flood Mitigation in New York
expert_rss.summary: FEMA has announced nearly $2 million in funding to New York for long-term projects that will make local communities more resilient to floods.

expert_rss.title: COLUMN: The Myth of the Perfect Message in Emergency Management
expert_rss.summary: In crisis communications, we still behave as if the right words can rescue a wrong situation.

expert_rss.title: Armed Forces Medical Examiner System Supports Artemis II Mission
expert_rss.summary: The Armed Forces Medical Examiner System provides critical forensic pathology and contingency logistics for NASA's crewed launches to ensure immediate medical and investigative readiness in the event of a mishap.

expert_rss.title: Eyes, Ears of Joint Force: Caine Honors Unsung Intelligence Community Heroes
expert_rss.summary: Successful military operations depend on those in the intelligence community, including the Defense Intelligence Agency, National Security Agency, National Reconnaissance Office, Central Intelligence Agency and others, said Air Force Gen. Dan Caine, chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff.

expert_rss.title: Traumatic Brain Injury Field Assessment Program Could Revolutionize Treatment
expert_rss.summary: The Defense Health Agency Operational Medical Systems Program Management Office helps mature the development of traumatic brain injury detection capabilities and accelerates the successful completion of the Traumatic Brain Injury Field Assessment Program.

expert_rss.title: Joint Force Enforces Maritime Blockade in Gulf of Oman, Globally
expert_rss.summary: The Navy is enforcing a growing global blockade to seize ships doing business with Iran and has authorized lethal force to protect maritime interests.

expert_rss.title: Ukraine’s Drone-Hunting An-28 Turboprop Is Now Launching Interceptor Drones
expert_rss.summary:
The latest evolution of Ukraine’s rugged An-28 Cash turboprop counter-drone platform combines interceptor drones and Minigun armament.

expert_rss.title: HH-60W Combat Rescue Helicopters To Take On Doomsday Evacuation Role In The Nation’s Capital
expert_rss.summary:

expert_rss.title: Augmented reality brings Revolutionary War to life at Army Museum
expert_rss.summary: “American Revolution: The Augmented Exhibition” will remain open until at least July 2027.

expert_rss.title: Remains of USS Arizona crew buried as unknowns after Pearl Harbor to be identified
expert_rss.summary: While the advocacy group Operation 85 met the 60% DNA threshold last November, the DPAA has finally confirmed its effort.

expert_rss.title: A century of the ‘Ma Deuce’: How the M2 Browning became America’s workhorse machine gun
expert_rss.summary: The highly versatile weapon has seen action affixed to the wings of P-51D Mustangs, aboard vessels in Vietnam and atop Humvees in the Middle East.

expert_rss.title: Ukraine to field 25,000 ground robots in push to replace soldiers for frontline logistics
expert_rss.summary: Ground robots ran more than 9,000 frontline missions in March, triple November's count, and captured their first Russian position last summer.

expert_rss.title: US Air Force aims to double F-15EX Eagle fleet
expert_rss.summary: The U.S. Air Force plans to boost the number of F-15EX Eagle II aircraft in its inventory to 267.

expert_rss.summary: The sailor was transferred back to Japan for medical care, a Navy spokesperson said.
